featureName: BUG-001 Shell-Heavy Reality Scan Discovery Gap

executionHistory.summary: Created BUG-001 to track the framework reality-scan discovery gap that blocks Scope 05 certification for a legitimate shell-heavy implementation inventory.

Reconciled the bug-owned packet after the repaired upstream source surface in bubbles/scripts/implementation-reality-scan.sh was restored from the clean baseline, patched for shell-heavy discovery and bounded harvesting, and revalidated by the current-session selftest/live scan/framework-validation chain. Validate-owned certification and closure remain pending.

Implementation review confirmed the repaired source surface already contained the required shell-heavy discovery fix, so no further code change was needed in this session before regression reruns continued.

Test execution reran the dedicated selftest, the live feature scan, release-manifest generation and selftest, and the broader framework validation chain; the test phase was exercised twice in this session and evidence was recorded in report.md.
Regression verification confirmed the shell-heavy discovery fix stayed green while the adversarial missing-inventory case continued to fail honestly with ZERO_FILES_RESOLVED.
A narrow cleanup landed in bubbles/scripts/implementation-reality-scan.sh during this session, after which the packet evidence was rerun and remained green.
Stabilization reruns confirmed the repaired discovery path remained compatible with the live feature packet and the broader framework command surface.
Security-oriented scan coverage inside the implementation reality scan reruns completed without introducing new violations for the repaired shell-heavy discovery path.
Validation ran against the repaired packet and surfaced that execution/provenance state still reflected the early blocked stub, leaving validate-owned certification closure pending this state repair.
Audit confirmed scanner behavior was clean and narrowed the remaining incompatibility to bug-owned execution provenance and guard-facing state layout, not to the repaired implementation surface.

scopeProgress.name: Upstream Discovery Support For Shell-Heavy Inventories

notes: Validate-owned certification now records the packet as done after artifact lint, traceability, state-transition, and framework validation all passed in the current session. The upstream source script remains the repaired implementation-bearing surface, and no certification blocker remains open for this bug packet.
